L1.area: CLI plugin layer for server stack configuration and service provisioning

L2.description: A TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race condition exists where a configuration file containing sensitive service credentials is first created with default permissions using a standard file open call, and then restrictive permissions are applied in a separate operation afterward. An attacker who knows the timing of program execution could read or modify the file contents during the window between file creation and permission change.

L3.area: MySQL service configuration file generation within the server stack setup plugins
L3.description: A TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race condition exists where a MySQL configuration file containing sensitive database credentials is first created with default permissions using a standard file open call, and then restrictive permissions are applied in a separate operation afterward. An attacker who knows the timing of program execution could read or modify the sensitive database configuration contents during the window between file creation and permission change.

ground_truth.reason: The pre_pref function creates a MySQL configuration file with open() using default (world-readable) permissions and only sets restrictive permissions via a separate chmod call afterward, creating a TOCTOU race window where an attacker could read or modify the sensitive configuration.
